methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $57M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Space & Aerospace. Range: $1.0B - $3.5B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 49.8x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Perigee Aerospace is a Series D space and aerospace company that has raised $56.64M to date. The valuation analysis triangulates two applicable methods across a peer set of 3 same-stage private companies (Astranis, Apex Space, Stoke Space) valued between $1.0B and $3.5B, alongside 5 public comparables in the Space & Aerospace sector.

The stage-progression method applies a 1.5x step-up multiplier to total raised ($57M proxy), implying a valuation of $85M. The peer-median approach across the 3 Series D space companies yields $1.0B, reflecting the upper-quartile valuations in the frontier aerospace market. A public-comp P/S anchoring method is not applicable due to absence of revenue disclosure. These two methods produce a wide implied range reflecting inherent dispersion in frontier-tech valuations at this stage.

The triangulated analysis supports a range of $72M (low) to $1.1B (high), with a median of $542M. The substantial gap between the stage-progression floor and peer median reflects the early-stage cost basis versus market pricing for proven execution in the space sector. Key uncertainties include Perigee's specific technological differentiation relative to Astranis and Stoke Space, revenue trajectory visibility, and capital requirements to next milestone. The peer median likely anchors valuation absent disclosed financial metrics, though downside scenarios weighted toward stage-progression multiples remain defensible given funding history.
